subject: GRB 240831B: Fermi GBM Final Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

"At 17:02:38.47 UT on 31 August 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM)
triggered and located GRB 240831B (trigger 746816563/240831710).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data,
is RA = 284.49, Dec = 47.35 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 18h 58m, +47d 21'),
with a statistical uncertainty of 16.75 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 18 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240831710/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn240831710.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240831710/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn240831710.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240831710/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn240831710.gif"
